title: How Sri Lanka Is responding to the Israel–Iran conflict: Day 5
byline: As tensions escalate between Israel and Iran, Sri Lanka has taken a series of diplomatic, aviation and precautionary measures to manage potential fallout and safeguard its citizens. Here is how Sri Lanka is responding.

title: Allen’s 33-ball Ton fires New Zealand into T20 World Cup Final
byline: New Zealand national cricket team powered into the T20 World Cup final in ruthless fashion on Wednesday night, crushing South Africa national cricket team by nine wickets in a one-sided semi-final in Kolkata.

title: Extend hospitality beyond borders: Timely opportunity for Sri Lanka’s tourism: Former Diplomat
byline: With number  of tourists currently stranded in Sri Lanka due to the ongoing international flight disruptions, former diplomat Kananathan has called on authorities to adopt a proactive and compassionate response to safeguard the country’s tourism reputation and future investment prospects.

title: CID yet to report facts to court on detention of retired Major General Suresh Sallay
byline: Colombo Fort Magistrate Isuru Neththikumara stated today that the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) has not yet reported facts to Court regarding retired Major General Suresh Sallay, who is currently being detained and questioned under the Prevention of Terrorism Act.

title: 87 bodies of crew in sunken Iranian ship recovered: Minister
byline: At least 87 bodies of those who had been on board the Iranian ship which sank off Sri Lankan waters have been recovered, Minister of Foreign Affairs Vijitha Hearth said.

title: 109 Sri Lankans stranded in Saudi & 22 in Oman Airports
byline: At least 109 Sri Lankans are stranded in Saudi Arabia Airport while 22 were stranded in Oman Airport, Minister of Foreign Affairs Vjitha Herath said while assuring that some of them might have already returned to Sri Lanka.

title: US carried out strike on Iranian warship off Sri Lanka coast, officials say
byline: The U.S. military carried out a strike ​that sank an Iranian warship off Sri Lanka’s coast and triggered ‌a rescue for dozens of Iranian sailors by Sri Lanka’s navy, three U.S. officials told Reuters on Wednesday.

title: President telephones UAE President; expresses sadness over recent attacks
byline: President Anura Kumara Dissanayake telephoned President of the United Arab Emirates Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed to express his sadness over the recent attacks suffered by the UAE.

title: Spain’s Sanchez doubles down on stance against US-Israeli attack on Iran despite Trump’s threats
byline: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro ‌Sanchez on Wednesday doubled down on his opposition to the attack on Iran by the United States and Israel, warning that the conflict risked triggering a major global disaster.

title: Iran postpones Khamenei’s funeral citing anticipated massive crowds
byline: Iran has delayed the state funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, originally scheduled for Wednesday evening in Tehran, citing expectations of an exceptionally large public turnout, according to state television.

title: Iranian warship off Sri Lanka ‘attacked by foreign submarine’- Sources
byline: The Iranian warship that sank off Sri Lanka’s southern coast had been attacked by a foreign submarine while it was outside Sri Lankan waters, defense sources confirmed to Daily Mirror.

title: Sri Lanka should not become a victim or intermediary in Middle East war: Minister
byline: Cabinet Spokesperson and Minister Dr. Nalinda Jayatissa said that Sri Lanka should never become an intermediary or a victim in the ongoing Middle East conflict.

title: AG undertakes to update court on human-elephant conflict measures
byline: The Attorney General today gave an undertaking before the Court of Appeal to inform the Court of the progress on the steps taken by the authorities, including the implementation of a National Action Plan to mitigate the human-elephant conflict in the country.

title: Ravi K questions Govt over prolonged low inflation
byline: UNP MP Ravi Karunanayake raised serious concerns over Sri Lanka’s continued below-target inflation, questioning whether official economic indicators reflect the rising cost of living faced by the public.

title: Was Iranian ship hit within Sri Lanka’s exclusive economic zone?: Namal questions
byline: SLPP MP Namal Rajapaksa today questioned whether the Iranian ship was hit within Sri Lanka’s Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).

title: Defence, Public Security to absorb bulk of 15,839 public sector jobs
byline: The bulk of the 15,839 public sector vacancies approved by the Cabinet of Ministers will be absorbed by the defence and public security sectors, according to details of the latest recruitment drive.

title: Tight security deployed at tourist centres amid Middle East crisis
byline: Tourist arrival centres, including Israeli Chabad Centres across the country, have been placed under heightened security in view of the ongoing Middle East crisis, Cabinet Spokesman and Minister Nalinda Jayatissa said.

title: Over 100 missing after Iranian warship sinks outside Sri Lankan waters
byline: More than 100 crew members are missing, and over 75 are wounded after an Iranian warship sank outside Sri Lankan territorial waters, about 25 miles south of the island, early this morning, authorities said.

title: Japan to grant 8 low-floor buses to Sri Lanka
byline: Sri Lanka will receive eight used low-floor buses from the Japanese Government as a grant, following discussions on supporting the country’s public bus transport sector.

title: Govt moves to curb crop damage, human-wildlife conflicts
byline: The government has taken steps to tackle the growing problem of crop damage and human-wildlife conflicts, which have severely affected rural agricultural livelihoods and national food security.

title: Cabinet approves phased maize imports to sustain Thriposha production
byline: Sri Lanka Thriposha Limited will be allowed to import up to 15,000 metric tons of maize in 2026 following Cabinet approval, ensuring uninterrupted production of the fortified nutritional product.

title: Sri Lanka to phase out import cess to boost export competitiveness
byline: The government has approved a plan to phase out the cess imposed on imported goods over a four-year period from 2026 to 2029, aiming to enhance the competitiveness of local products in international markets.

title: SLTDA takes policy decision not to promote LGBTIQ tourism in Sri Lanka - AG
byline: The Attorney General today informed the Court of Appeal that the Sri Lanka Tourism Development Authority (SLTDA) has taken a policy decision not to promote or develop LGBTIQ tourism in Sri Lanka.

title: Britain halts student visas for four countries
byline: The British government announced Tuesday that it would stop issuing education visas to nationals from Afghanistan, Cameroon, Myanmar and Sudan and work visas for Afghans as part of its broader clampdown on asylum seekers.

title: South Korea woman and doctors guilty of murder after killing newborn baby
byline: A South Korean court has convicted a woman of murder after she delivered a baby that was later killed.

title: IMF official flags inflation, growth risks from Middle East war
byline: IMF First Deputy Managing Director Daniel Katz warned Tuesday that the escalating conflict in the Middle East carries significant risks for the global economy, particularly in terms of inflation and growth, but emphasized that it remains too early to assess the full extent of the impact.

title: Sri Lanka Navy, Air Force rescue 30 from Iranian ship sinking off Galle: Minister
byline: Sri Lanka Navy and the Sri Lanka Air Force have rescued some 30 persons who were inside the ship, which is sinking off Sri Lankan waters, Minister Vijitha Hearth told Parliament today.

title: Bill to administer SLC to be presented to Parliament
byline: A Bill pertaining to the reorganisation of Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) is to be presented to Parliament soon.

title: Brazilian President invited to visit Sri Lanka
byline: Brazilian President Lula da Silva has been invited to visit Sri Lanka, Prime Minister Harini Amarasuriya told Parliament today.

title: Air quality reaches unhealthy levels in several cities, NBRO warns
byline: The National Building Research Organization (NBRO) has reported that air quality in parts of the country has deteriorated to unhealthy levels. Areas including Kolonnawa, Kotte, Kegalle, and Galle have recorded particularly high readings.
